String.description: An immutable sequence of UTF-16 characters representing text. Kotlin strings support template expressions via $variable and ${expression} syntax, raw strings using triple quotes, and a rich set of extension functions for searching, transforming, and formatting text content.

length.description: Returns the number of characters in this string.

substring.signature: fun substring(startIndex: Int, endIndex: Int = length): String
substring.description: Returns a substring starting at startIndex and ending before endIndex.

split.signature: fun split(vararg delimiters: String, ignoreCase: Boolean = false, limit: Int = 0): List<String>
split.description: Splits the string around matches of the given delimiters and returns a list of substrings.

replace.signature: fun replace(oldValue: String, newValue: String, ignoreCase: Boolean = false): String
replace.description: Returns a new string with all occurrences of oldValue replaced with newValue.

trim.description: Returns a string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

toIntOrNull.description: Parses the string as an integer in the given radix, returning null if the string is not a valid integer.

contains.signature: fun contains(other: CharSequence, ignoreCase: Boolean = false): Boolean
contains.description: Returns true if this string contains the specified other sequence of characters.

List.description: An ordered, read-only collection of elements that supports indexed access. Lists are one of the most commonly used collection types in Kotlin, created via listOf() for immutable or mutableListOf() for mutable variants, and support rich functional operations via extension functions.

get.description: Returns the element at the specified index, throwing IndexOutOfBoundsException if out of range.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the list.

contains.description: Checks if the list contains the specified element using equals comparison.

indexOf.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the element, or -1 if not found.

filter.description: Returns a list containing only elements matching the given predicate function.

map.description: Returns a list containing the results of applying the transform function to each element.

sortedBy.signature: fun <T, R : Comparable<R>> Iterable<T>.sortedBy(selector: (T) -> R?): List<T>
sortedBy.description: Returns a list of all elements sorted by the value returned by the selector function.

first.description: Returns the first element, throwing NoSuchElementException if the list is empty.

Map.description: A read-only collection of key-value pairs where each key is unique. Created via mapOf() for immutable or mutableMapOf() for mutable maps. Kotlin maps support destructuring declarations, filtering, mapping, and efficient key-based lookup.

get.description: Returns the value for the given key, or null if no such key exists in the map.

keys.description: Returns a read-only set of all keys in this map.

values.description: Returns a read-only collection of all values in this map.

entries.description: Returns a read-only set of all key-value pairs in this map.

containsKey.description: Returns true if the map contains the specified key.

getOrDefault.signature: fun getOrDefault(key: K, defaultValue: V): V
getOrDefault.description: Returns the value for the given key, or the defaultValue if no mapping exists.

Set.description: A read-only collection of unique elements with no defined ordering guarantee. Created via setOf() for immutable or mutableSetOf() for mutable variants. Supports set-theoretic operations like union, intersect, and subtract via extension functions.

contains.description: Checks if the set contains the specified element.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the set.

union.description: Returns a set containing all elements from both this set and the other collection.

intersect.description: Returns a set containing only elements present in both this set and the other collection.

subtract.description: Returns a set containing elements in this set but not in the other collection.

Sequence.description: A lazily evaluated collection that computes elements on demand during iteration. Unlike eager List operations, Sequence chains intermediate operations without creating temporary collections, making it efficient for large data sets or expensive transformations. Created via sequenceOf(), generateSequence(), or the .asSequence() extension.

filter.description: Returns a sequence containing only elements matching the given predicate (lazy evaluation).

map.description: Returns a sequence that applies the transform function lazily to each element.

take.description: Returns a sequence containing only the first n elements.

toList.description: Returns a List containing all elements of this sequence, triggering terminal evaluation.

Pair.signature: data class Pair<out A, out B>(val first: A, val second: B)
Pair.description: A generic data class representing a pair of two values. Created using the infix function to (e.g., 1 to "one"). Commonly used for map entries, returning two values from a function, and destructuring declarations via (a, b) = pair syntax.

first.description: The first element of the pair.

second.description: The second element of the pair.

toList.description: Converts this pair into a list containing the first and second elements.

Regex.signature: class Regex(pattern: String, options: Set<RegexOption> = emptySet())
Regex.description: Represents a compiled regular expression pattern used for matching, finding, and replacing text. Supports named groups, regex options like IGNORE_CASE and MULTILINE, and provides Kotlin-idiomatic APIs for pattern operations that integrate cleanly with string extension functions.

find.signature: fun find(input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int = 0): MatchResult?
find.description: Returns the first match of the regular expression in the input, starting at startIndex, or null if not found.

findAll.signature: fun findAll(input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int = 0): Sequence<MatchResult>
findAll.description: Returns a lazy sequence of all non-overlapping matches of the pattern in the input string.

matches.description: Returns true if the entire input string matches this regular expression pattern.

replace.signature: fun replace(input: CharSequence, replacement: String): String
replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of the pattern in the input with the replacement string.

containsMatchIn.description: Returns true if there is at least one match of this pattern anywhere in the input string.

println.description: Prints the given message to the standard output stream followed by a newline character. Accepts any nullable type, converting it to a string representation via toString(). This is the primary function for console output in Kotlin programs.

listOf.description: Creates an immutable list containing the specified elements in the order they are provided. The returned list does not support add or remove operations. Use mutableListOf() when mutation is needed. This is the idiomatic Kotlin way to create read-only lists.

data class.signature: data class Name(val prop1: Type1, val prop2: Type2, ...)
data class.description: Declares a class whose primary purpose is holding data, automatically generating equals(), hashCode(), toString(), copy(), and componentN() destructuring functions from the primary constructor parameters. Data classes must have at least one val or var parameter and cannot be abstract, open, sealed, or inner.

Flow.description: An asynchronous data stream that sequentially emits values and completes normally or with an exception.

collect.description: Terminal operator that collects the given flow.

map.description: Returns a flow containing the results of applying the transform.

filter.description: Returns a flow containing only values matching the predicate.

first.description: The first element emitted by the flow.

toList.description: Collects all elements into a List.

onEach.description: Returns a flow that invokes the given action before each value.

catch.signature: fun catch(action: suspend FlowCollector<T>.(cause: Throwable) -> Unit): Flow<T>
catch.description: Catches exceptions in the flow completion.

launch.signature: fun CoroutineScope.launch(context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext, start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit): Job
launch.description: Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to the coroutine as a Job.

async.signature: fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext, start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): Deferred<T>
async.description: Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of Deferred.

withContext.signature: suspend fun <T> withContext(context: CoroutineContext, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T
withContext.description: Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context, suspends until it completes, and returns the result.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
